title: validAccessToken()
summary: Returns the stored access token.
rawIndexableTextContent: Returns the stored access token.  Discussion pCloud tokens do not expire, so this simply returns the stored token without any refresh logic. Called by PCloudCloudFileSystem before each API request.

title: PCloudAuthenticator
summary: Manages OAuth 2.0 authentication for pCloud.
rawIndexableTextContent: Manages OAuth 2.0 authentication for pCloud.  Overview Handles the OAuth 2.0 implicit grant (token) flow: opening the browser, capturing the access token from the redirect URI fragment, and storing the token in the Keychain. pCloud access tokens do not expire, so there is no refresh logic. Once authorized, the stored token remains valid indefinitely (or until the user revokes it in the pCloud web console). Interactive authorization requires AuthenticationServices and is available on iOS 16+ and macOS 13+. Keychain storage works on all platforms including tvOS and watchOS. Setup Create an app at my.pcloud.com under My Applications Note your App Key (Client ID) and set a Redirect URI Configure the authenticator:  The authenticator automatically selects the correct API endpoint (US or EU) based on the hostname returned during authorization. Threading This class is @unchecked Sendable. Mutable state (stored credentials) is accessed only from async methods that are serialized by callers.

title: init(accessToken:apiBaseURL:)
summary: Creates a pCloud cloud file system with a static access token. The app is responsible for managing the token externally.
rawIndexableTextContent: Creates a pCloud cloud file system with a static access token. The app is responsible for managing the token externally.  OAuth 2.0 access token obtained via pCloud’s auth flow. API base URL. Defaults to the US endpoint.

The correct endpoint is determined automatically during authorization.

rawIndexableTextContent: A pCloud-based cloud backend for Ensembles. Overview EnsemblesPCloud provides PCloudCloudFileSystem, an implementation of the Ensembles CloudFileSystem protocol that stores sync data on pCloud. It communicates directly with pCloud’s REST API using URLSession — no third-party SDK required. pCloud is a Swiss cloud storage provider subject to Swiss data privacy laws, making it a strong choice for privacy-conscious applications. The backend supports both US and EU API endpoints for data residency requirements. Authentication pCloud uses OAuth 2.0. Your app is responsible for completing the OAuth flow and obtaining an access token. Pass the token when creating the file system:  Setup  API Details pCloud supports path-based access on all endpoints, so Ensembles paths map directly without any ID resolution. The backend uses these pCloud API methods: Operation pCloud Endpoint Connect / Identity userinfo File existence stat Create directory createfolderifnotexists List directory listfolder Remove file deletefile Remove directory deletefolderrecursive Upload uploadfile (multipart) Download getfilelink + GET  Error Handling pCloud API errors are mapped to EnsembleError cases: Result code 1000 (login required) and 2003 (access denied) map to EnsembleError/authenticationFailure. Result codes 2000, 2005, and 2009 (not found) map to EnsembleError/fileAccessFailed. All other errors map to EnsembleError/serverError. Dependencies This module has no external dependencies beyond Foundation and the core Ensembles framework. All network communication uses URLSession.
title: EnsemblesPCloud
summary: A pCloud-based cloud backend for Ensembles.

rawIndexableTextContent: A cloud file system backed by pCloud’s REST API using URLSession.  Overview This backend communicates directly with pCloud’s REST API — no third-party SDK is required. All authentication is handled via an OAuth 2.0 access token passed as a query parameter on every request. pCloud supports path-based access on all endpoints, so Ensembles paths map directly to pCloud paths without any ID resolution or caching. Two initialization paths are available:  By default the US API endpoint (https://api.pcloud.com) is used. For European data residency, pass the EU endpoint:  When using PCloudAuthenticator, the correct endpoint is selected automatically based on the user’s account region. Setup Create an app at my.pcloud.com under My Applications Note your App Key (Client ID) and set a Redirect URI Use PCloudAuthenticator for the full OAuth flow, or obtain a token manually from the pCloud developer console for testing  Threading This class is @unchecked Sendable. All network calls go through a shared URLSession instance, which is thread-safe. Mutable state (loggedIn) is only written during connect() and error handling.
title: PCloudCloudFileSystem
summary: A cloud file system backed by pCloud’s REST API using URLSession.

title: authorize(presenting:)
summary: Performs interactive OAuth 2.0 authorization using ASWebAuthenticationSession.
rawIndexableTextContent: Performs interactive OAuth 2.0 authorization using ASWebAuthenticationSession.  The presentation anchor (UIWindow on iOS, NSWindow on macOS). Discussion Opens the pCloud sign-in page using the implicit grant (token) flow. The access token and API hostname are returned directly in the redirect URI fragment — no token exchange step is needed.

title: redirectURI
summary: The redirect URI registered in the pCloud developer console.
rawIndexableTextContent: The redirect URI registered in the pCloud developer console.

title: fetchUserIdentity()
summary: Fetches a token representing the current user of the cloud file system. Often this will be the user or login name.
rawIndexableTextContent: Fetches a token representing the current user of the cloud file system. Often this will be the user or login name.
